Abstract

along with broadcasting of additional signals, offers more options for Precise Point Positioning (PPP). Utilizi

PPP‑AR solution
To carry out our PPP-AR study, we used GNSS data on 1
April 2024 (92nd day of the year) at 100 stations for UPD/
OSB estimation and 120 stations for PPP-AR verification.
Their geographic distribution is shown in Fig. 5. To gen-
erate phase OSB, muti-frequency and muti-GNSS pseu-
dorange and carrier phase are jointly processed in UDUC
PPP for the base stations. After groupin

Multi-frequency and multi-GNSS PPP-RTK method, we
developed a grid points model with a 0.5-degree interval
using Kriging Interpolation Methods (KGR), as indicated
by the gray points in the figure. To obtain a clean and
accurate ionosphere, we utilized only the fixed satellite
solution. Additionally, the satellite with the highest eleva-
tion angle was chosen as the reference for inter-satellite
single differencing, effectively eliminating the impact of
receiver hardware delays. Blue t

Fig. 13 Global map of daily average PODP for different constellations
From Fig. 16 and Table 8, both in terms of RMS values
and convergence time, whether in single-system or multi-
system scenarios, atmospheric constraints significantly
enhance positioning performance, largely compensating
for slow convergence speeds in the situations with insuffi-
cient satellite numbers or poor geometric configurations.
In such cases, the convergence time for single-system
solutions is around 7 min, while multi-system solutions
can achieve nearly i

Z hang et al. Satellite Navigation (2025) 6:17 Page 17 of 23
Figure 19 displays the positioning errors. It shows that
the float solution exhibits significant fluctuations. Due
to the lower accuracy of the float solution, PPP-AR has
fewer fixed satellites leading to low fix rate. However,
with the implementation of atmospheric constraints,
PPP-RTK demonstrates a substantial reduction in posi-
tioning errors and is capable of maintaining high accu-
racy even with a limited number of satellites. Table 10
presents the relevant metrics used in the PPP calcula-
tions. Compared to float and fixed sol
